definition: The BLS methodology lag of 12-18 months will mechanically deliver 40-80bp of CPI relief as shelter decelerates from ~5.0% to ~3.5-4.0%, but this is necessary and insufficient for returning to 2%, with steady-state shelter inflation structurally higher than pre-pandemic due to a 3.5-5.5M unit housing supply deficit.
evidence: Private rent indices (Zillow ~3.0-3.5% YoY) lead CPI shelter by 12-18 months
Shelter at ~36% of CPI, deceleration contributes 40-80bp of headline disinflation
1983 and 1995 precedents show OER-driven disinflation masked ongoing services ex-shelter inflation
Steady-state shelter inflation structurally higher at ~3.5-4.0% vs. pre-pandemic ~3.0%
open_questions: Will core CPI printing below 2.5% on OER drag trigger a premature 'mission accomplished' narrative (the 1995 trap)?
How much of the housing supply deficit is resolvable within the policy-relevant horizon?
